initial_request_score.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: SEMpIMe: a tool for integrating DNA methylation effects in transcription factor binding affinity predictions
SEMpIMe uses ChIP-seq and whole genome bisulfite sequencing to predict effects of methylation within binding sites.
Here we introduce SEMpIMe, a computational tool to generate predictions of the effect of methylation on transcription factor binding strength in every position within a transcription factor's motif.
generating predictions that encompass the magnitude and direction of change to transcription factor binding for all 4 nucleotide base pairs

issues.reason: Paper title and abstract use 'SEMpIMe', but body text and abbreviations list consistently use 'SEMplMe'. Extraction follows title/abstract.

initial_request_score.fix_suggestion: No fix needed; extraction matches title and abstract. Note internal paper inconsistency between title (SEMpIMe) and body (SEMplMe).

evidence.quote: SEMpIMe functions as an extension of our previously published expectation–maximization method, SEMpl [17].
Using SEMpl all kmers with a PWM score under the TFM-P VALUE threshold are generated

SEMpIMe validates known methylation sensitive and insensitive positions within a binding motif, identifies cell type specific transcription factor binding driven by methylation, and outperforms SELEX-based predictions for CTCF.
